name: de Young Museum

permits.description: erect museum & research building 3 stories, 2 basements
to erect 2 story museum & 9 story tower
erect an one story type 5 construction recreation and office
to erect 1 story nursery w/ green house and a3 assembly area in golden gate park.
install shoring for excavation, excavate and stock spoils, compact for new building, cut foundation

photo_credit.author: Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco (FAMSF)

summary_en: The de Young's civic record centers on rebuilding the museum in Golden Gate Park. A 2000 draft environmental impact report and 2001 revisions cover the project to demolish and reconstruct the M.H. de Young Memorial Museum and its Education Tower. A 1997 site-selection study weighs a Golden Gate Park versus a downtown location, and a 1997 analysis studies the museum's visitorship and fiscal benefit. Fine Arts Museums board minutes from 2000 and 2004 track the "My de Young" fundraising campaign and resolutions naming donor galleries, while a 1970–71 annual report records a van Gogh exhibition at the de Young seen by over 400,000 people. The museum is operated by the Fine Arts Museums, whose FY2025 budget line totaled about $24M.

documents.title: New de Young Museum : revisions to final environmental impact report
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Planning Dept
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: 2001 revisions to the final EIR for the New M.H. de Young Museum, addressing the reduced height of the Education Tower and the shadow and visual analyses.

documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: The 2000 draft EIR for the project to demolish and reconstruct the de Young in Golden Gate Park, removing about 230,000 sq ft of existing buildings.

documents.gloss: The 1970–71 annual report records the de Young's van Gogh exhibition, seen by over 400,000, and gifts received by the M.H. de Young Memorial Museum.
